objective: Make phase progression depend on validated attempt artifacts, archive active artifacts before reruns, pass latest archived JSON as structured rerun context, and keep phase transitions consistent.
goals: Force-advance to the next phase when required markdown and JSON artifacts are produced and validation passes.
Archive active artifacts before rollback or same-phase rerun.
Pass the latest archived JSON artifacts to the agent as rerun context.
Keep approval, rollback, retry, and normal phase transitions consistent across run-scoped and task-scoped phases.

root_findings.title: cli.ps1 is a control-plane monolith
evidence: Invoke-EngineStep orchestrates recovery, archive, prompt assembly, execution, validation, and event/state commits.
Test-PhaseArtifactCompletion, Resolve-StepValidation, New-EnginePromptText, and Sync-RunStateFromCanonicalArtifacts live in the same entrypoint file.
impact: Small changes couple transition logic, artifact handling, and provider invocation.
Phase-transition bugs are hard to isolate and test.

root_findings.title: Phase success is decided by inconsistent mechanisms
evidence: Post-run validation uses Resolve-StepValidation on canonical artifacts.
Final mutation still depends on Apply-JobResult(job.result_status, validation.valid).
impact: Valid artifacts can still lead to failed runs.
Provider fast-exit and timeout paths behave differently.

root_findings.title: Canonical active artifacts are both work-in-progress and source of truth
evidence: Providers write directly into runs/<run>/artifacts/run or tasks.
Validators and output sync logic read the same active paths.
impact: Partial writes and stale artifacts can contaminate decisions.
Archive-before-rerun reduces risk but does not create attempt isolation.

root_findings.title: Validation mutates canonical artifacts
evidence: Resolve-StepValidation normalizes artifacts and writes them back with Save-Artifact.
impact: Provider mistakes and engine repairs are hard to distinguish.

root_findings.title: Archived rerun context is prompt text, not structured state
evidence: There is no first-class archived_context_refs object in job state.
impact: Agents must parse prompt text to find rerun context.
The exact archived snapshot used by a rerun is not tracked structurally.

root_findings.title: Provider transport is brittle and string-concatenation based
evidence: execution-runner.ps1 builds a single ProcessStartInfo.Arguments string.
copilot provider uses argv prompt transport with -p.
Current runtime failure: too many arguments. Expected 0 arguments but got 66.
impact: Prompt quoting and size issues block normal phase execution.
Transport failures leak into phase-transition behavior.

root_findings.title: Phase cursor is advanced around dispatch rather than commit
evidence: Approval resolution updates the run cursor to the proposed dispatch target.
Dispatch writes running state and active_job_id before phase completion is committed.
impact: run-state, events, and user-facing status can describe different realities.
Phase history is harder to interpret.

root_findings.title: Compatibility projection is coupled to canonical commit
impact: Legacy compatibility paths interfere with canonical workflow logic.

principles: Validated attempt artifacts are the source of truth for phase success.
Provider exit is a transport signal, not the primary completion signal.
Canonical active artifacts contain only committed outputs.
Rerun context is structured data, not path text embedded in prompts.

components.name: PhaseAttemptStore
components.responsibility: Allocate attempt-scoped staging directories and attempt metadata.

components.name: PhaseArchiveService
components.responsibility: Archive active artifacts before rerun and record snapshot metadata.

components.name: PhaseValidationPipeline
components.responsibility: Validate required artifacts in staging and return normalized outputs without mutating canonical state.

components.name: PhaseCompletionCommitter
components.responsibility: Promote validated staging artifacts to canonical active storage atomically and emit completion events.

components.name: JobContextBuilder
components.responsibility: Assemble selected task, open requirements, input refs, and archived rerun context into structured job context.

components.name: ProviderTransportAdapter
components.responsibility: Expose prompt transport capabilities such as stdin, argv, temp_file, or protocol.

workstreams.title: Split orchestration logic out of cli.ps1
Introduce attempt-scoped staging and atomic commit
deliverables: atomic promotion to canonical active paths

workstreams.title: Make archive-before-rerun deterministic
deliverables: archive decision policy based on attempt creation
archive metadata with previous_job_id and previous_attempt_id
empty active directory before rerun dispatch

workstreams.title: Turn archived JSON rerun data into structured context
deliverables: prompt rendering from structured rerun context
traceability from rerun attempt to archive snapshot

workstreams.title: Refactor provider transport and fix Copilot execution
Clarify the phase state machine
deliverables: clear distinction between current committed phase and dispatched phase
phase history based on attempt lifecycle

workstreams.title: Expand scenario-based regression coverage
deliverables: valid artifacts + failed provider exit still advances phase
rerun archives old outputs and passes archived JSON context

acceptance_criteria: When required markdown and JSON artifacts exist in staging and validation is valid, the run advances to the next phase even if the provider exits non-zero.
Before rollback or same-phase rerun, existing active canonical artifacts are archived and removed from the active phase directory.
Rerun job metadata contains structured archived_context_refs pointing to the latest archived JSON snapshot.
Validator does not mutate canonical artifacts directly.
show, run-state.json, and events.jsonl describe the same phase progression.
primary_risk: Applying archive-before-rerun alone without attempt-scoped staging and commit semantics will keep reintroducing stale-read and fast-exit inconsistencies.
conclusion: The fundamental fix is to combine attempt-scoped staging, validation-before-commit, structured archived context, and provider transport refactoring. Without these together, the same transition inconsistencies will recur.
